## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

{rbrv_regime}
conjuring vessels.
Two complementary roles depending on whether the slot's
{rbrv_image_anchor}
is set:
+
- *When the slot is open (no ANCHOR yet)*: ORIGIN is the upstream registry tag the vessel author intends to mirror (e.g., `python:3.11-slim`). {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} consumes it as the source for `skopeo inspect`/`copy`.
- *When the slot is pre-populated with a hallmark-base ANCHOR*: ORIGIN is informational — a lineage marker (typically the producing vessel's sigil, e.g., `rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge`). {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} ignores ORIGIN under skip-if-set; conjure resolves the base from
{rbrv_image_anchor}.
+
The Dockerfile uses `ARG RBF_IMAGE_n` / `FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_n}` -- the Foundry substitutes the resolved reference via `--build-arg` at conjure time.

[[rbrv_image_anchor]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_locator
{rbrv_image_anchor}::
{rbst_locator}
naming a GAR-resident base image for
{rbrv_regime}
conjuring vessels.
The locator's namespace identifies how the slot was populated:
+
- `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` — written by
{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}.
The anchor itself is `«sanitized-origin»-«10-char-sha256»` (e.g., `python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`); the locator embeds it as both path segment and tag.
Enshrined images are stored as peer packages under the shared `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` namespace, not per-vessel — vessels sharing the same base image origin share one enshrined artifact.
- `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/«BASENAME»:«HALLMARK»` — written by an orchestrating procedure (handbook step or onboarding-sequence fixture) when the vessel's base image is an internal hallmark (e.g., the airgap-bottle building FROM the just-ordained forge image).
The locator points at the specific
{rbtga_ark_image}
inside the producer hallmark's subtree.
{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}

file.content:
===== Ifrit Bottle Vessel

The Ifrit ships as a three-vessel family under `rbev-vessels/`:

* `rbev-bottle-ifrit-tether` — Tether-built variant for daily kludge iteration. Built directly from upstream Debian + cargo.
* `rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap` — Airgap-built variant produced from enshrined bases without upstream pulls. Same runtime image as the tether variant; differs only in supply-chain provenance.
* `rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` — Build-time forge fixture that warms the cargo cache and pre-stages apt deps so the airgap build has no remote dependencies. Consumed input, not a runtime image.

The two runtime variants share their source tree and Dockerfile family in `rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/` (`Dockerfile.tether` builds against the tether pool; `Dockerfile.airgap` uses the forge as `FROM` base). The forge itself lives in `rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-forge-context/`.

The Ifrit bottle is purpose-built for adversarial testing:

content.text:
Found prose 'forge' uses concentrated in onboarding handbook bash. One false positive ruled out: `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:460` is `fire-and-forget`, not 'forge'.

**Legitimate identifier uses (not targets):**
- `rbhoda_director_airgap.sh:39` — `z_forge_vessel="rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge"` (var def)
- `rbhoda_director_airgap.sh:91` — `${z_forge_vessel}` argument (link yawp)
- `rbhoda_director_airgap.sh:172, 192` — `${z_forge_vessel}` passed to `buh_tt` (tabtarget arg display)
- `rbhoda_director_airgap.sh:202, 210, 214` — sigil/var shown inside `buh_code` (literal config samples)
- `rbhoda_director_airgap.sh:39` line + the `FORGE_HALLMARK` env var in the code samples

**Prose targets in `rbk/rbh0/rbhoda_director_airgap.sh`:**

Comments:
- `22–23` — supply-chain header comment ("conjure forge tethered, conjure airgap ifrit from enshrined forge")

Description strings:
- `34` — `buc_doc_brief "${RBHO_TRACK_AIRGAP} — enshrine, conjure forge, conjure airgap, charge moriah, compare plumb"`

User-facing prose (`buh_line` / `buh_step1`):
- `109` — "Build a project-authored toolchain image (the forge) ${RBYC_TETHERED}"
- `110` — "Build the airgap ${RBYC_BOTTLE} ${RBYC_AIRGAP} from the enshrined forge"
- `150` — "(forge → …)" name-shift gloss
- `163` — "The forge ${RBYC_VESSEL} ${z_lk_forge} declares its upstream base in"
- `170` — "Run ${RBYC_ENSHRINE} on the forge:"
- `174` — "${RBYC_ENSHRINE} resolves the upstream base for the forge ${RBYC_VESSEL}"
- `180` — "${RBYC_CONJURE} the forge ${RBYC_TETHERED}, then point the airgap ${RBYC_BOTTLE} at it"
- `182` — "The forge is a project-authored toolchain image. It pre-stages"
- `186` — "Build it on the ${RBYC_TETHERED} pool — the forge ${RBYC_RBRV} declares:"
- `195` — "forge is toolchain plumbing that your customer code will be"
- `200` — "forge as its base:"
- `206` — "the locator pointing at the forge's just-built ${RBYC_HALLMARK} inside"
- `207` — "your ${RBYC_DEPOT}'s hallmark namespace. ${RBYC_ORDAIN} wrote the forge"
- `212` — "Open the airgap ${RBYC_RBRV} and set ANCHOR to the forge image's locator:"
- `219` — "${RBYC_ENSHRINE} sees the populated ANCHOR, HEAD-validates that the forge"
- `226` — "Airgap base anchor populated — forge is ready to serve as airgap base"
- `228` — "${RBYC_CONJURE} the forge, then write the locator into the airgap ${RBYC_RBRV}"
- `236` — "pre-staged in the enshrined forge."
- `238` — "The airgap ${RBYC_VESSEL}'s Dockerfile starts FROM the forge:"
- `363` — "${RBYC_CONJURE} the forge ${RBYC_TETHERED}, re-${RBYC_ENSHRINE} its ${RBYC_HALLMARK}"
- `364` — "${RBYC_CONJURE} the final ${RBYC_BOTTLE} ${RBYC_AIRGAP} from the enshrined forge"

**Prose targets in `rbk/rbh0/rbho0_start_here.sh`:**
- `137` — "then a project-authored toolchain image (the forge) — then build the"
- `141` — "${RBYC_CONJURE} the forge ${RBYC_TETHERED}, then re-${RBYC_ENSHRINE}"

**Prose target in `rbk/rbz_zipper.sh`:**
- `94` — `buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_AIRGAP "rbw-Oda" … "Airgap Cloud Build — enshrine, conjure forge, conjure airgap, charge moriah, compare plumb"` (mirrors line 34 of rbhoda)

**Patterns to notice for tightening:**
- The airgap track is the one place where 'forge' carries semantic load distinct from the sigil — it's filling a role ("project-authored toolchain image", "airgap's FROM base") that has no current quoin. If you mint a quoin for the role (e.g., `rbyc_forge` as a linked term parallel to `rbyc_bottle`/`rbyc_vessel`), most of the `rbhoda_director_airgap.sh` prose collapses to `${RBYC_FORGE}` substitutions and the bare-word 'forge' disappears.
- The recurring "${RBYC_CONJURE} the forge ${RBYC_TETHERED}" / "from the enshrined forge" pairings appear three times verbatim (109/110, 363/364, plus the `buc_doc_brief`/`buz_enroll` descriptions at 34 and zipper:94) — once a quoin lands, those duplicate strings are obvious candidates for a single helper or constant.
- `rbho0_start_here.sh:137,141` and the comment block at `22–23` are phrased identically to the user-facing prose — same fix applies.

Want me to draft the quoin (RBYC linked term + RBS0 attribute) and a sample edit on one of the heavier `buh_line` clusters?

See if you can see actual quoins that fit for each forge use you see.  This is a 'found' topic, and thus reeks of word cancer that appears when I don't stop you from being creative at the wrong time.

lastPrompt: I have let you edit docs a lot without carefully reviewing, and I think somehow you started to think forge was a good key idea.  I strongly disagree!  See if you can see actual quoins that fit for eac…

aiTitle: Replace forge with better prose terms in RBS0

content.text:
Re-examining: 'forge' is indeed not load-bearing. It's a colloquial gloss for "this specific vessel that the airgap track happens to use as its base." The system already has the right quoins.

**The concept inventory for each prose 'forge' use:**

- **The thing called 'forge'** is a vessel (sigil `rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge`) → `${RBYC_VESSEL}` exists, and `${z_lk_forge}` already provides the sigil link in `rbhoda_director_airgap.sh`
- **The artifact 'the forge produces/the forge image'** is a hallmark → `${RBYC_HALLMARK}` exists
- **The role 'serves as base for another vessel'** is already expressible via "base" + the `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` mechanic; no quoin needed because no concept is being named — it's just a relationship
- **The sigil itself when literal identity matters** belongs in backticks or via `${z_lk_forge}`

So no quoin is missing. Every prose 'forge' is one of: redundant gloss on a vessel already named, anaphoric ("the forge" where "it" works), or a sigil-as-identifier that belongs in code formatting.

**Per-use mapping (`rbhoda_director_airgap.sh`):**

| Line | Current | Replaces with |
|------|---------|---------------|
| 22–23 (comment) | "conjure forge tethered, conjure airgap ifrit from enshrined forge" | "conjure the base vessel tethered, conjure airgap ifrit from its enshrined hallmark" — comment, plain English fine |
| 34 / zipper:94 | "enshrine, conjure forge, conjure airgap, …" | "enshrine, conjure base, conjure airgap, …" — just drop the proper-noun pretense |
| 109 | "a project-authored toolchain image (the forge)" | "a project-authored toolchain `${RBYC_VESSEL}`" — drop the gloss |
| 110 | "from the enshrined forge" | "from its enshrined `${RBYC_HALLMARK}`" |
| 150 | "(forge → ...)" | sigil literal in backticks if name-shift is the point; otherwise drop the parenthetical |
| 163 | "The forge `${RBYC_VESSEL}` `${z_lk_forge}` declares…" | "The `${z_lk_forge}` `${RBYC_VESSEL}` declares…" — already has both, drop bare 'forge' |
| 170 | "Run `${RBYC_ENSHRINE}` on the forge:" | "Run `${RBYC_ENSHRINE}` on `${z_lk_forge}`:" |
| 174 | "the upstream base for the forge `${RBYC_VESSEL}`" | "the upstream base for the `${z_lk_forge}` `${RBYC_VESSEL}`" — or drop, antecedent clear |
| 180 | "`${RBYC_CONJURE}` the forge `${RBYC_TETHERED}`" | "`${RBYC_CONJURE}` `${z_lk_forge}` `${RBYC_TETHERED}`" |
| 182 | "The forge is a project-authored toolchain image." | recast — antecedent established at 180; "It pre-stages…" |
| 186 | "the forge `${RBYC_RBRV}` declares:" | "its `${RBYC_RBRV}` declares:" |
| 195 | "forge is toolchain plumbing…" | "it is toolchain plumbing…" |
| 200 | "forge as its base:" | "this `${RBYC_VESSEL}` as its base:" or restructure |
| 206 | "the forge's just-built `${RBYC_HALLMARK}`" | "the just-built `${RBYC_HALLMARK}`" — antecedent clear |
| 207 | "`${RBYC_ORDAIN}` wrote the forge…" | "`${RBYC_ORDAIN}` wrote its `${RBYC_HALLMARK}`…" (need to read full sentence) |
| 212 | "the forge image's locator" | "that `${RBYC_HALLMARK}`'s locator" |
| 219 | "validates that the forge…" | "validates that the base `${RBYC_HALLMARK}`…" |
| 226 | "forge is ready to serve as airgap base" | "base `${RBYC_VESSEL}` is ready" — or drop ("airgap base anchor populated" is already complete) |
| 228 | "`${RBYC_CONJURE}` the forge" | "`${RBYC_CONJURE}` `${z_lk_forge}`" |
| 236 | "pre-staged in the enshrined forge." | "pre-staged in its enshrined `${RBYC_HALLMARK}`." |
| 238 | "Dockerfile starts FROM the forge:" | "Dockerfile starts FROM that `${RBYC_HALLMARK}`:" |
| 363 | "`${RBYC_CONJURE}` the forge `${RBYC_TETHERED}`" | "`${RBYC_CONJURE}` `${z_lk_forge}` `${RBYC_TETHERED}`" |
| 364 | "from the enshrined forge" | "from its enshrined `${RBYC_HALLMARK}`" |

**`rbho0_start_here.sh`:**
- 137 "(the forge)" — drop the parenthetical; "a project-authored toolchain `${RBYC_VESSEL}`" stands alone
- 141 "`${RBYC_CONJURE}` the forge `${RBYC_TETHERED}`" — sigil link or just "the base `${RBYC_VESSEL}`"

**RBS0:3215 / RBSAE:27** — illustrative parenthetical inside a structural definition. The 'forge' adds nothing; a real-vessel example is `rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` literal, or — better — make the example generic: "the airgap-bottle building FROM another vessel's just-ordained hallmark."

**RBSIP:84, 86** — RBSIP is the one place a sigil-roster genuinely names this vessel. The bare-word 'forge' usages there are still redundant gloss ("Build-time forge fixture" → "Build-time fixture vessel"; "uses the forge as FROM" → "uses `rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` as FROM"; "The forge itself lives" → "It lives").

The pattern across all sites: the word 'forge' was acting as a pseudo-quoin without an anchor — exactly the word-cancer signature. No mint needed; the surgery is replacement with existing quoins or anaphora.

local -r z_moniker="moriah"
  local -r z_tether_moniker="tadmor"
  local -r z_forge_vessel="rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge"
  local -r z_airgap_vessel="rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap"
  local -r z_tether_vessel="rbev-bottle-ifrit-tether"
  local -r z_airgap_rbrv="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_airgap_vessel}/rbrv.env"
  local -r z_moriah_rbrn="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
  local -r z_tether_rbrn="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_tether_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"

  local z_has_director=0
  local z_secrets_dir=""
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    z_secrets_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR") || z_secrets_dir=""
  fi
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
    z_has_director=1
  fi

  local z_has_depot=0
  if test -f "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"; then
    local z_line=""
    while IFS= read -r z_line; do
      case "${z_line}" in RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=?*) z_has_depot=1; break ;; esac
    done < "${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  fi

  local z_airgap_base_enshrined=0
  if test -f "${z_airgap_rbrv}"; then
    local z_anchor=""
    z_anchor=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_airgap_rbrv}" "RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR") || z_anchor=""
    test -n "${z_anchor}" && z_airgap_base_enshrined=1
  fi

  local z_airgap_ordained=0
  if test -f "${z_moriah_rbrn}"; then
    local z_moriah_hallmark=""
    z_moriah_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_moriah_rbrn}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_moriah_hallmark=""
    case "${z_moriah_hallmark}" in
      ""|PENDING-*) ;;
      *) z_airgap_ordained=1 ;;
    esac
  fi

  local z_tether_ready=0
  local z_tether_hallmark=""
  if test -f "${z_tether_rbrn}"; then
    z_tether_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_tether_rbrn}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_tether_hallmark=""
    case "${z_tether_hallmark}" in
      ""|PENDING-*) z_tether_hallmark="" ;;
      *) z_tether_ready=1 ;;
    esac
  fi

  buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Vessel"    "${z_forge_vessel}";   local -r z_lk_forge="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Vessel"    "${z_airgap_vessel}";  local -r z_lk_airgap="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Nameplate" "${z_moniker}";        local -r z_lk_moriah="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Nameplate" "${z_tether_moniker}"; local -r z_lk_tadmor="${z_buym_yelp}"

  buh_section "${RBHO_TRACK_AIRGAP} — Your Own Supply Chain"
  buh_e
  buh_line "In ${RBHO_TRACK_FIRST_BUILD} you ${RBYC_ORDAIN}ed a ${RBYC_VESSEL} on the"
  buh_line "${RBYC_TETHERED} pool — Cloud Build pulled base images from the"
  buh_line "public internet during the build. ${RBYC_AIRGAP} removes that"
  buh_line "dependency: zero network during the build, every input pre-staged"
  buh_line "in your ${RBYC_DEPOT}."
  buh_e
  buh_line "This track builds ${z_lk_airgap} — the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} variant that"
  buh_line "matches the ifrit you met in the ${z_lk_tadmor} adversarial suite,"
  buh_line "now with full supply-chain discipline. The chain has three links:"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  1. Mirror the rust base from upstream into your ${RBYC_DEPOT} (${RBYC_ENSHRINE})"
  buh_line "  2. Build a project-authored toolchain image (the forge) ${RBYC_TETHERED}"

content.text:
`z_lk_forge` is a local-readonly yelp fragment in `rbhoda_director_airgap.sh:91` produced by:

```
buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Vessel" "${z_forge_vessel}"
local -r z_lk_forge="${z_buym_yelp}"
```

It's a hyperlinked string: **display text** `rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` (the sigil) → **anchor target** `Vessel` on the public docs page. The `z_lk_` prefix is the file's local convention for "link yelp" captures, paralleling `z_lk_airgap`, `z_lk_moriah`, `z_lk_tadmor` on lines 92–94.

Compared to `${RBYC_VESSEL}`:

| | display text | link target |
|---|---|---|
| `${RBYC_VESSEL}` | "Vessel" | docs#Vessel |
| `${z_lk_forge}` | "rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge" | docs#Vessel |

Same anchor, different display. `${RBYC_VESSEL}` shows the concept; `${z_lk_forge}` shows the specific sigil while still linking to the concept page.

Implication for the prose-'forge' surgery: `${z_lk_forge}` is the right replacement only when the sentence is identifying *that particular vessel by its sigil*. Where the sentence is talking about "a vessel" in role-terms, `${RBYC_VESSEL}` (with antecedent already established earlier in the buh_line block) is the cleaner fit. My earlier table over-recommended `${z_lk_forge}` in spots where antecedent + `${RBYC_VESSEL}` would read better — the sigil only needs to appear once or twice per section to anchor identity, not at every reference.

content.text:
Notched as `ae039b87` on ₣BA.

## Skipped — non-cosmetic / pedagogical

**`rbhoda_director_airgap.sh` L150–151** — `"The two ${RBYC_VESSEL} names shift mid-track (forge → / airgap), so vessels appear as literals in commands."`

The bare 'forge' and 'airgap' here are sigil shorthands acting as illustrative names. Replacing only 'forge' breaks parallelism; replacing both with full sigils makes the parenthetical clunky; 'airgap' as a literal also collides with the egress-mode quoin `RBYC_AIRGAP`. Cleanest fix is probably to delete the metalinguistic explanation entirely (the sentence is teaching about a UI quirk that may not deserve airtime), but that's a content decision, not cosmetic.

**`rbhoda_director_airgap.sh` L210, L214** — `FORGE_HALLMARK` env var name in learner-facing `buh_code` samples (`export FORGE_HALLMARK=...`, `RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=rbi_hm/${FORGE_HALLMARK}/image:${FORGE_HALLMARK}`).

You explicitly carved out names-in-bash. This is one — learners type it, it's a real shell variable. Renaming changes pedagogy. Candidates if you want to reconsider: `BASE_HALLMARK` or `TOOLCHAIN_HALLMARK` (parallel to your "base"/"toolchain" framing in the prose around it now).

## Borderline edits I went ahead with — flag for sanity check

**L34 `buc_doc_brief` and `rbz_zipper.sh` L94** — changed "conjure forge" → "conjure base" in the short tabtarget descriptions shown to users in `--help`/listings. Not pure cosmetic since it changes summary language; I matched both for consistency. Easy to revert if you'd rather keep "forge" as a colloquial milestone label in those summaries.

**L182 / L194–195 definitional sentences** — "The forge is a project-authored toolchain image" → "It is a project-authored toolchain image that pre-stages..." and the parallel L194–195. These restructure with anaphora rather than re-naming. Antecedent was already established in the step header above (which now uses `${z_lk_forge}`), so no information lost — but worth a glance since they're declarative rather than referential.

## Identifiers preserved (untouched, intentionally)
- vessel sigil `rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge` everywhere it appears literally
- `z_forge_vessel`, `z_lk_forge` local var names
- `FORGE_HALLMARK` env var (see above)
- `common-ifrit-forge-context/` source directory references
- `Dockerfile.airgap` / `Dockerfile.tether` filenames

The classifier conflated two qualitatively different jobs into a single tabtarget: (1) mirror upstream + write back ANCHOR (forge case), (2) HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch was wrong-direction; the right move was to remove the overload so each job has a clean home. The HEAD-200 'skip' path was framed as an optimization but actively breaks enshrine's contract: HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's earlier commit 14242011 records empirically when rust:slim-bookworm digest moved 5ae2d2ef98 to caaf9ca7ac). rbfd_enshrine now: (a) requires at least one slot has RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN populated (ANCHOR-only is no longer a valid input shape; ANCHOR is a derived artifact of this operation, not a pin); (b) unconditionally submits the Cloud Build job that inspects current upstream, computes the content-addressed locator from the live manifest digest, and copies into GAR; (c) extract overwrites whatever was previously committed in RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR. Re-runs are idempotent w.r.t. upstream state, not w.r.t. the file's prior contents — that distinction was the original design mistake. Internal pipeline: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots removed entirely (84 lines), zrbfd_enshrine_submit's classification-file read loop replaced with direct ${RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN:-} reads. Empty ORIGIN slots become empty CB substitutions; the CB step's existing `test -n ${ORIGIN} || continue` guard skips them naturally. Orchestration: rbtdro_onboarding.rs drops the enshrine-airgap-validate call after the airgap-bottle anchor write; the forge hallmark's existence in GAR is established by ordain-forge's success above, no separate validation step needed. airgap_sigil local removed (now unused). Block comment + docstring updated to remove skip-if-set framing. Commit-message string in source: removed coronet prefix from new commit string per directive (coronets should not appear in source). Pre-existing coronet-prefixed commit strings elsewhere in this file (BBAA0/BBAAu) are left as-is; broader sweep is follow-up work. These are documentation drift, deferred to a follow-up notch per operator pause. da698def jjb:1011-f365fe89:₢BBAAs:n: BBAAs revert f365fe89 (enshrine-classifier-stale-cache-regenerate). The four-way classifier patch added more branching to a switch that exists only because BBAA0 overloaded enshrine to be both 'mirror upstream + write back' (forge case) and 'HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark' (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch is wrong-direction; the right move is to remove the overload. Operator-driven reframing: the classifier's HEAD-200 'skip' path is not an optimization — it actively breaks enshrine's contract. Enshrine's job is to compute the current correct ANCHOR for the current upstream; HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest forever, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's commit 14242011 records empirically: 5ae2d2ef98 → caaf9ca7ac when upstream digest moved). The next notch implements the simplification: drop the entire classifier, make rbfd_enshrine always submit the CB job, remove the orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call (the hallmark-pin's existence is established by ordain-forge's success, not by a separate validation step), partial-revert BBAA0 spec prose accordingly. b4062af8 jjb:1011-7cb6a2c6:₢BBAAs:n: BBAAs enshrine-classifier-stale-cache-regenerate: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots conflated two qualitatively different cases when RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR was populated and HEAD returned 404 — orchestration-written hallmark-pin (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN empty) and committed-derived cache (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN set, e.g. forge vessel's rust:slim-bookworm enshrinement). BBAA0's skip-if-set design fail-fasted on both with 'caller wrote an unresolvable locator', which is correct for the pin case but wrong for the cache case: ORIGIN is the source of truth and the stale ANCHOR is a derived value that can be regenerated.
